fact: November 7 is exactly 311 days into the year, which means there are only 54 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is prime time for squirrels to go absolutely nuts burying acorns - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most northern cities around this date, making it feel like bedtime when you're barely done with homework.
November 7 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season, when the mysterious scorpion rules the zodiac from October 23 to November 21.
Maple trees in cold regions are dropping their last colorful leaves right now, creating nature's most satisfying crunchy carpet.
This date sits almost perfectly between the autumn equinox and winter solstice - like the exact middle of fall's countdown to winter.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 7 means spring is heating up and jacaranda trees are exploding with purple flowers.
The first frost of the year typically hits many northern areas around this time, turning spider webs into glittery ice sculptures overnight.
Deer are in their most active mating season right now, which means they're running around like crazy and making weird snorting sounds.
November 7 is when the Taurid meteor shower often peaks, sending slow-moving fireballs streaking across the night sky.
Bears in cold climates are entering their final feeding frenzy before hibernation, eating up to 20,000 calories per day - that's like 40 hamburgers!
